WATER AUTHORITY PROCUREMENT

Solicitation Title: Water Quality and Environmental Monitoring Data Platform
Procurement Reference: WA-2026-ENVIRONMENT-021
Contracting Authority: Thames Valley Water Authority
Published Date: 5 February 2026
Closing Date: 2 April 2026 13:00
Estimated Contract Value: £3.1 Million
Contract Duration: 30 months

BACKGROUND

Thames Valley Water Authority manages water resources, quality monitoring, and environmental compliance across a 4,200 square mile region. Current monitoring data is scattered across legacy systems operated by multiple suppliers, preventing real-time insight into water quality trends and environmental impacts.

PROJECT OBJECTIVES

Data Integration:
- Consolidate data from 180+ water quality monitoring stations
- Integrate 50+ upstream catchment monitoring sites (Environment Agency partnership)
- Connect wastewater treatment facility data (15 sites)
- Include historical data (10 years) for trend analysis
- Support real-time telemetry from automated sensors (1-hour update frequency)

Water Quality Analytics:
- Real-time monitoring of key parameters (dissolved oxygen, pH, turbidity, temperature)
- Detection of pollution incidents and automated alert system
- Algal bloom prediction using environmental data (temperature, nutrients, light)
- Tracking of pesticides and pharmaceutical residues (compliance monitoring)
- Nutrient cycle analysis (nitrogen, phosphorus) for regulatory reporting

Environmental Impact:
- Habitat impact assessment for biodiversity reporting
- Flow regime analysis for ecological health
- Fish passage and barrier assessment tools
- Integration with ecological survey data (bird, invertebrate populations)

Regulatory Compliance:
- Automated reporting for Environment Agency requirements (WFD, Habitats Directive)
- Drinking water quality compliance tracking
- Wastewater discharge permit monitoring
- Conservation site status and trigger alerting

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS

1. Real-time data ingestion from 180+ water monitoring stations
2. Support 50+ concurrent users across multiple sites
3. Alert generation within 15 minutes of anomaly detection
4. 99.8% system availability during operations
5. Historical data archive (10 years) with query capability
6. Automated Environment Agency compliance reporting
7. Integration with 5 different legacy monitoring systems
8. Data quality assurance with automatic validation rules

TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS

- Cloud or on-premises deployment (water industry infrastructure considerations)
- Real-time streaming data architecture (sub-minute latency for alerts)
- Time-series database for sensor measurements
- Geospatial capability for site mapping and catchment analysis
- Advanced analytics for trend detection and forecasting
- Mobile app for field inspector access to real-time data

OPTIONAL ENHANCEMENTS

- Machine learning for pollution source identification
- Integration with weather services for predictive analytics
- Drone or satellite imagery integration for rapid assessment
- Integration with public app for river condition reporting
- Automated sampling optimization (reduce frequency for stable parameters)

SCORING

Technical Solution and Innovation: 40%
Experience with Water/Environmental Systems: 30%
Regulatory Compliance Capability: 20%
Cost and Value for Money: 10%

Submit to: procurement@thamesvalleywater.gov.uk
